Fine to proceed toward tomorrow's lock with this included in the May close package.\", \"user\": \"npc_016\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_reviewer_approves_entries_and_lock_path_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780507920.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From tax, the May revaluation piece reads as a non-cash translation gain only. I do not see a federal deduction-timing implication from the GBP payment timing here, so this should stay a book treatment question rather than a tax timing item.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_019_orphan_exception_peer_and_tax_breadcrumbs_exist_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780507800.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"JE shape looks right from the prepaid schedule side: DR Prepaid Facilities Maintenance 435.41 and CR FX Translation Gain 435.41. If we book it in FP-2026-06, the prepaid amortization schedule will pick up the revised carry value automatically on the next monthly run, so no manual catch-up line needed.\", \"user\": \"persona_014\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780497720.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_019_orphan_exception_peer_and_tax_breadcrumbs_exist_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780500240.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I pulled the AP autoroute history after your question. Datadog has been default-routing into 500000 across FY25, and the cumulative misclassification is roughly $340K. Presentation-wise that is material to gross margin, but it is immaterial to net income since it is all within opex/COR classification. I'm drafting a SAB 99 quant memo for Andrea so the close package can support a go-forward-only treatment unless she wants a broader retrospective cleanup.\", \"user\": \"persona_009\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_preparer_quantifies_misclassification_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780498440.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Thanks for catching this. Do we know whether Datadog has been hitting 500000 for just May or for a few prior months as well? If it has been miscoded for a while, I want to understand whether we need a prior-period adjustment or if a go-forward correction in May is sufficient. Mainly trying to gauge whether the gross margin presentation issue is material enough to require more than a cleanup this month.\", \"user\": \"npc_016\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780412880.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_028_monthly_close_reviewer_questions_prior_period_scope_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780497720.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Need same-day approval help on BlackLine exception exc_cb0a9a94a3084c tied to recon BL-3978BDB68290 for account 132000 Prepaid Facilities Maintenance. James Randall is the approver on it and the SLA is June 4. Jones and I landed on a corrective JE in FP-2026-06 rather than treating the $435.41 as translation noise. Ben Arinzo, can you sanity-check the prepaid amortization schedule shape against the proposed JE? Hannah Grant, can you weigh in on the GBP payment timing tax angle from the February 2026 payment on the London corporate-services prepaid block? People identified:
- **Ryan Delgado** (Audit Senior) — *identified* the exception and flagged the "feed drop / June BD2 retry" premise.
- **Daniel Jones** (Accounts Manager) — the **sign-off** ("Daniel Jones is OK with accept-timing").
- **Blue Evans** (Accounts Associate) — exception **assignee**, who asked me to check.
- **George McAdam** — gave the FP-2025-11 precedent. **Hannah Grant** — tax view.

The June FP-2026-06 retry has **no run record at all** (no failed batch exists to retry). Let me find Blue's original request to me and confirm George/Hannah emails so I can reach everyone correctly.

input.payload:
Before we lock exc_aade06f6129e43 / BL-333FF9956BC6 (102000 Cash - Payroll, -$28.59) as accept-timing: I post the payroll cash entries on this account, so I checked it against the systems and the feed-drop basis doesn't hold up.

• Feed history, FP-2026-05: the only feeds that post to 102000 both ran clean. brookfield_payroll_feed = 330 rows in / 330 posted / 0 rejected (run_1fb45b81237648, success). brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed = 2,616 / 2,616 / 0 rejected (success). No feed dropped any rows into this account this period — there's no "29 dropped rows" in the run record.

• GL 102000 had zero activity in May: opening = closing = $2,192,357.22, debits 0 / credits 0, no transactions. Nothing posted to the account and nothing is missing from the GL, so there are no dropped rows that "fed the account."

• June BD2 retry: there is no failed or partial May batch to retry, and no FP-2026-06 run exists. The May feed already fully succeeded, so a retry can't clear anything. The exception SLA (2026-06-01) has already lapsed with no retry posting.

• Support: nothing is attached to the recon or the exception (no attachments, no supporting evidence, nothing in Records Vault). The recon's own recorded explanation actually reads "FX revaluation rates refreshed after the period's closing snapshot" — a different cause from the feed-drop story. The narratives on the table don't agree with each other or with the systems.

• Precedent (FP-2025-11): the cited "$42 feed-drop accepted as timing, cleared by the next retry" doesn't match the record. The Nov 102000 recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) variance was -$3.42 — within the $10 threshold, certified clean, no feed-drop note, no exception, no retry dependency. The only real payroll-feed failure was Oct FP-2025-10 (connection reset, 3,298 rows rejected, INC3827486) — different period, full failure, not a small residual cleared by a Nov retry.

Net: the -$28.59 is a book-vs-supporting difference on a static account (same shape as last period's -$3.42), not a feed drop, and there is no retry that will make it disappear. content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and who I squared it with.

## The variance
**BL-333FF9956BC6 — account 102000 Cash - Payroll, FP-2026-05.** GL $2,192,357.22 vs supporting $2,192,385.81 = **−$28.59**. Tracked as **exception exc_aade06f6129e43** (type "subledger_feed_drop," still in *logged* state, unsigned).

## The stories being told (they don't line up)
- **Ryan Delgado** (raised it / exception `identified_by`): "feed dropped **29 expected rows** that fed 102000… **June BD2 retry already queued**… accept-timing." This is the premise everything rests on.
- **Blue Evans** (assignee, opened the #monthly-close-coordination triage thread): relayed that framing, proposed accept-timing pending the June retry — but privately asked me to check, which is why we're here.
- **George McAdam**: precedent — "same pattern on 102000 in **FP-2025-11**, **$42** feed-drop residual accepted as timing, next retry cleared it."
- **Hannah Grant**: payroll-tax downstream is immaterial "unless the retry misses again" (defers to the retry premise).
- **Daniel Jones**: signed off — "OK with locking disposition as accept-timing."
- And the recon's **own recorded explanation** (auto-attributed to me) says something else entirely: **"FX revaluation rates refreshed after the period's closing snapshot."** Three different causes on one item.

## What the systems actually show — the feed-drop read falls apart
- **Every feed that posts to 102000 ran clean in May.** `brookfield_payroll_feed` = 330 in / 330 posted / **0 rejected** (success). `brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed` = 2,616 / 2,616 / **0 rejected** (success). There is no "29 dropped rows" anywhere in the run record.
- **GL 102000 had zero activity all period** — opening = closing = $2,192,357.22, debits 0 / credits 0, no transactions. Nothing fed the account and nothing is missing from the GL, so there are no dropped rows to recover.
- **No June retry exists to clear it.** There's no failed/partial May batch and no FP-2026-06 run — the May feed already fully succeeded. The exception's own SLA (2026-06-01) has already lapsed with nothing posted.
- **No support is attached** — recon attachments empty, exception evidence empty, nothing in Records Vault. The feed-drop narrative has zero backing, and the "FX revaluation" note is equally unsupported (102000 is a USD cash-payroll funding account, per Hannah's own point).
- **The precedent is misstated.** FP-2025-11's 102000 recon (BL-8DCA6908E272) variance was **−$3.42, not $42** — within the $10 threshold, certified clean, **no feed-drop note, no exception, no retry**. The only real payroll-feed failure was **Oct FP-2025-10** (connection reset, 3,298 rows rejected, INC3827486) — a different period and a full failure, not a small residual a Nov retry cleared.

**Bottom line:** the −$28.59 is a genuine **book-vs-supporting difference on a static account** (same shape as last period's −$3.42), not a feed drop. There is no retry that will make it disappear, and the precedent doesn't justify a timing waive. It needs a real explanation / corrective entry before any disposition.

## Who I squared it with (each where they raised it)
1. **#monthly-close-coordination triage thread** (the close thread it's running on) — posted the full evidence reply, tagging George, Hannah, and Blue.
2. **Blue Evans & Ryan Delgado DM** (where the recommendation originated) — laid out why the feed-drop + June-retry premise fails and asked to hold.
3. **Daniel Jones** (the sign-off) — direct message asking him to hold the accept-timing sign-off, with the evidence.
4. **Reminder set** for you (June 13, 08:57) to chase this before the period certifies so it doesn't quietly lock on the wrong basis.

**One caveat to flag:** the Slack thread reply posted under George McAdam's account identity rather than yours (the messaging tool's default user) — the content is clearly in your voice ("I post the payroll cash entries on this account"), but if you want it unambiguously attributed to you, you may want to add a one-line note in-thread. The two messaging DMs and the reminder went out correctly.
